sectionPatterns.label: Centered Clear Offer Hero
sectionPatterns.description: Centered hero with strong heading, short support copy, visible CTA row, and optional trust chips below.
avoidIf: business needs product screenshot first
hero needs side-by-side visual storytelling

sectionPatterns.label: Split Hero with Visual Panel
sectionPatterns.description: Left side copy with CTA, right side visual panel or image area with trust chips or supporting visual treatment.
avoidIf: business has no visual support at all
product needs a software UI first

sectionPatterns.label: Offer-Focused Hero with Side Panel
sectionPatterns.description: Main offer and CTA on one side, structured reassurance panel on the other side showing key reasons to act.
avoidIf: brand needs an ultra-minimal editorial hero
offer is very image-led

sectionPatterns.label: Trust-Panel Local Service Hero
sectionPatterns.description: Hero with clear service positioning and an adjacent trust panel containing service facts, chips, or reassurance points.
avoidIf: product-led SaaS hero
brand wants high editorial minimalism

sectionPatterns.label: Product-Led SaaS Hero
sectionPatterns.description: Value proposition and CTA on the left, product UI or dashboard visual on the right with optional floating proof cards.
avoidIf: service business without product UI
spiritual or ceremonial service

sectionPatterns.label: Simple Service Card Grid
sectionPatterns.description: A clean multi-card grid where each card explains one service clearly and concisely.
avoidIf: business has only one very custom service
highly editorial page with low card density

sectionPatterns.label: Icon List Services
sectionPatterns.description: Services presented as a structured list with icons or numbered markers, ideal for fast scanning.
avoidIf: business needs rich imagery
brand wants visually lush storytelling

sectionPatterns.label: Image Card Services
sectionPatterns.description: Cards with a visual top area and service copy beneath, useful when each service benefits from a more polished presentation.
avoidIf: business is extremely practical/utility-led
no visual support exists

sectionPatterns.label: Alternating Detail Rows
sectionPatterns.description: Each service or feature is presented in a more detailed row layout, useful when the offer needs explanation rather than just a short grid.
avoidIf: offer is very simple
mobile-first quick-scan utility page only

sectionPatterns.label: Story-Led Service Blocks
sectionPatterns.description: Service items feel more like narrative blocks, useful for higher-trust or higher-touch services.
avoidIf: page must be highly transactional
business is price/utility-led

sectionPatterns.label: List with Icons
sectionPatterns.description: Compact set of service or feature rows with leading icon/marker, good for dense but readable content.
avoidIf: brand needs highly visual cards
very expressive premium page

sectionPatterns.label: Benefits Icon List
sectionPatterns.description: A clean list of benefits with supporting descriptions, useful for explaining value quickly.
avoidIf: benefits need long narrative treatment

sectionPatterns.label: Alternating Benefit Rows
sectionPatterns.description: Benefit blocks arranged with more visual rhythm and slightly deeper content.
avoidIf: page is extremely short and practical

sectionPatterns.label: Single Highlight + Supporting Quotes
sectionPatterns.description: One large featured testimonial supported by a few smaller quotes, ideal when one emotional story should lead.
avoidIf: you only have one weak generic quote
page already has too many oversized blocks

sectionPatterns.label: Quote Grid
sectionPatterns.description: A grid of testimonial cards for broader proof coverage and quick scanning.
avoidIf: brand wants one emotionally strong focal story

sectionPatterns.label: Avatar Quote Cards
sectionPatterns.description: Friendly review cards with strong author presence, useful for human warmth and relatability.
avoidIf: brand is very formal and minimal
testimonials are highly corporate

sectionPatterns.label: Side-by-Side Proof Blocks
sectionPatterns.description: A tighter proof layout using paired testimonials or statements for controlled trust building.
avoidIf: page needs warmer emotional social proof

sectionPatterns.label: Centered Warm CTA Band
sectionPatterns.description: Centered CTA section with a strong emotional close and one or two actions.
avoidIf: page needs a more action-heavy utility CTA strip

sectionPatterns.label: Dual Action CTA Band
sectionPatterns.description: CTA band with two actions, such as Book Demo / Talk to Us or Book Now / WhatsApp.
avoidIf: brand should feel extremely minimal and quiet

sectionPatterns.label: Contact Strip CTA
sectionPatterns.description: Utility-oriented closing strip that reinforces contact channels and gives a low-friction next step.
avoidIf: page should end on a highly visual premium block

sectionPatterns.label: Simple Accordion FAQ
sectionPatterns.description: Straightforward accordion list for resolving user hesitation in a clean format.
avoidIf: you do not have enough meaningful FAQ items

sectionPatterns.label: Grouped FAQ Topics
sectionPatterns.description: FAQ organized into clearer categories such as process, pricing, eligibility, or support.
avoidIf: FAQ set is very small and simple

sectionPatterns.label: FAQ with CTA Close
sectionPatterns.description: FAQ section designed to lead naturally into the next CTA, useful when hesitation needs direct resolution before action.
avoidIf: CTA already appears too frequently nearby

sectionPatterns.label: Simple Utility Footer
sectionPatterns.description: Compact footer with business name, short tagline, limited nav links, and basic contact path.
avoidIf: business needs a more established corporate footer

sectionPatterns.label: Multi-Column Brand Footer
sectionPatterns.description: Richer multi-column footer with grouped links, stronger brand presence, and more complete navigation.
avoidIf: site is extremely small and single-page only
